solution_summary: Update the knowledge base search implementation so article matching uses contains/substring semantics instead of prefix-only checks. The change should remain localized to backend/app/services/knowledge_service.py, preserving existing search behavior for prefix matches while also returning articles when the query appears anywhere in topic, title, or content.
implementation_steps: Review the current search logic in backend/app/services/knowledge_service.py to identify any startswith/leading-anchored LIKE behavior or equivalent prefix-only checks.
Refactor the matching condition to use substring/contains matching across topic, title, and content fields, applying the same normalization path already used by search if present.
Preserve existing query normalization behavior unless it is directly responsible for prefix-only matching; avoid broad changes to routes, models, or schemas.
If the search is database-backed, adjust the query predicate from prefix matching to contains matching; if it is in-memory, replace startswith with an equivalent contains check.
Verify that the search still returns prefix matches and now also returns matches appearing mid-field in any of the three knowledge article fields.

planned_changes.change_summary: Change search filtering logic from prefix-only matching to substring/contains matching for topic, title, and content.
planned_changes.rationale: The issue is localized to the search method in this service, and updating this single file should satisfy the acceptance criteria with minimal blast radius.

patch_strategy: Make a narrow, behavior-focused change in the service layer only. First, identify the exact predicate used for search matching in the search method. Then replace any prefix-anchored comparison with a contains comparison while keeping the existing field list and normalization pipeline intact. If the implementation uses SQL or ORM filtering, translate the predicate to a wildcard-both-sides/contains equivalent; if it uses Python string checks, swap startswith for in. Avoid touching API contracts or data models unless tests reveal an unexpected dependency. Keep the change small enough to be reviewed as a single-file fix.
validation_steps: Add or update unit tests for the knowledge service search method to cover mid-string matches in content, title, and topic.
Add regression coverage proving prefix matches still work after the change.
Run the backend test suite focused on knowledge service/search tests, then run the full backend test subset if available.
Manually verify a sample query that appears only in the middle of an article returns that article, and that unrelated articles remain excluded.
pr_title: SDLC-21: Fix knowledge base search to match substrings in articles
pr_body:
## Summary
Fix knowledge base search so it returns articles when the query appears anywhere in the topic, title, or content, not just at the start of those fields.

## What changes
- Update search matching logic in `backend/app/services/knowledge_service.py`
- Preserve existing prefix match behavior
- Add coverage for mid-string matches

## Validation
- Unit tests for substring and prefix search behavior
- Manual verification of search results for mid-sentence matches

## Scope
- Localized backend service change only
risks: If the search implementation also performs relevance ranking, changing the predicate may slightly alter ordering for some queries.
If normalization is inconsistent across fields, substring matching could surface unexpected casing or punctuation edge cases.
If the query is translated to database LIKE/ILIKE, special characters may need escaping to avoid false positives.
assumptions: The search logic is indeed implemented in backend/app/services/knowledge_service.py as indicated by the Jira context.
Existing normalization behavior should be preserved unless it directly prevents substring matching.
No API response schema changes are required for this fix.
